It appeared to be what Ukrainians call a “double-tap attack,” a tactic that aims to kill emergency workers or firefighters responding to the first strike.

2022-10-23.abstract: The inner workings of the Chinese Communist Party are often hard to decipher, but the core organizational structure has not changed much in recent years.

2022-10-23.title: Xi is expected to unveil a new lineup of top officials.
2022-10-23.abstract: China’s most powerful leader in decades, Xi Jinping, is set to score a groundbreaking victory on Sunday, formally securing another term as head of the Communist Party and crowding the party’s top echelon with his protégés.

2022-10-23.title: How Political Primaries Drive Britain’s Dysfunction
2022-10-23.abstract: In the United States, too, the rise of inside-party primaries has empowered candidates at the extremes, and the result is likely to be a greater disconnect with the public.

2022-10-23.title: A loyal aide in Shanghai takes a leading role in Beijing.
2022-10-23.abstract: Li Qiang is now China’s second-highest official after Xi Jinping. Mr. Li’s time in Shanghai had raised questions about whether he would rise to the party’s highest levels.

2022-10-24.title: U.S. Executives Are Flocking to Saudi Davos in the Desert
2022-10-24.abstract: Some top American business leaders are headed to the Saudi business conference. But Biden administration officials, angry over the kingdom’s stance on oil production and ties with Russia, are staying away.

2022-10-24.title: As Israel Votes, Again, Netanyahu Pins Hopes on the Far Right
2022-10-24.abstract: To regain power, Benjamin Netanyahu, the longest-serving Israeli prime minister, will most likely need the support of Itamar Ben-Gvir, a far-right leader with a history of extreme views.

2022-10-24.title: Sunak was the clear front-runner.
2022-10-24.abstract: LONDON — Rishi Sunak, the former chancellor of the Exchequer, on Monday was set to become Britain’s next prime minister, after his last remaining rival dropped out of the race. The move will make Mr. Sunak the first person of color and the first person of Hindu faith to lead Britain.

2022-10-24.title: Brazil’s Polls Were Wrong. Now the Right Wants to Criminalize Them.
2022-10-24.abstract: President Jair Bolsonaro and conservative lawmakers in Brazil are trying to make it illegal to publish polls that later do not match the election results.

2022-10-24.title: Italy’s New Right-Wing Government Seeks to Affirm Support for Ukraine
2022-10-24.abstract: Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani expressed support for Ukraine, trying to dispel concerns that Italy could undercut Europe’s opposition to the Russian invasion.

2022-10-25.title: Dietrich Mateschitz, Creator of the Red Bull Empire, Dies at 78
2022-10-25.abstract: The Austrian salesman turned a Thai energy drink into a marketing juggernaut that owned sports teams, media outlets and a winning Formula 1 crew.

2022-10-25.title: 3 Palestinians Killed in Shootout With Israeli Forces, Palestinians Say
2022-10-25.abstract: Three Palestinians were killed during a firefight with Israeli security forces who had entered Nablus, a flash point city in the occupied West Bank, Palestinian officials said.

2022-10-25.title: How Bolsonaro Built the Myth of Stolen Elections in Brazil
2022-10-25.abstract: President Jair Bolsonaro has spent years undermining Brazil’s voting systems. Now the nation is bracing for his reaction if he loses Sunday’s election.

2022-10-25.title: Why People Are Flocking to a Symbol of Taiwan’s Authoritarian Past
2022-10-25.abstract: At a museum dedicated to Taiwan’s not-so-distant authoritarian past, Taiwanese see China’s present, and a dark vision of one possible future under autocratic rule.

2022-10-25.title: Three Inquiries, but No Answers to Who Blew Holes in Nord Stream Pipelines
2022-10-25.abstract: Denmark, Germany and Sweden are all investigating the ruptured pipeline sites, but they remain tight-lipped over who might have caused the damage and why.

2022-10-26.title: Ukrainians find devastation where Russians retreat.
2022-10-26.abstract: KAMIANKA, Ukraine — Little moved in the village of Kamianka, except for a cat bolting from under the rubble of a destroyed house and metal roofing banging in the wind.

2022-10-26.title: Natural gas prices are falling in Europe despite the war and dwindling supply.
2022-10-26.abstract: The war in Ukraine is raging, Russian natural gas exports to Europe are dwindling and the winter heating season is approaching. That would seem like a recipe for higher prices, yet the cost of the fuel, which is vital for heating homes and for powering electricity plants and industry, has been plummeting.

2022-10-26.title: Shunned by the West, Russia and Myanmar Form a Partnership of Unequals
2022-10-26.abstract: Myanmar gets resources and ammunition, while Russia gets a customer at a time when it is struggling for revenues. Each can use the other to undermine Western sanctions.

2022-10-26.title: In New Zealand, a 23-Million-Year-Old Fossil Is Carried Away by Parties Unknown
2022-10-26.abstract: The whale fossil, a cherished local attraction, was taken from a riverbank in broad daylight by two men with a rock saw and chisel, residents say.

2022-10-26.title: Labour Party Comes Out Swinging at Britain’s New Prime Minister, Rishi Sunak
2022-10-26.abstract: The opposition’s lines of attack, starting with Mr. Sunak’s choice of home secretary and his wife’s wealth, gave a glimpse of where it sees vulnerabilities in the governing Conservatives.

2022-10-27.title: Australian Judge Orders New Trial in Rape Case That Shook Capital
2022-10-27.abstract: A former parliamentary staffer is accused of raping a colleague in the defense minister’s office. The judge said a juror had brought an article on sexual assault cases into the jury room against her instructions.

2022-10-27.title: Putin Wants to Divide Ukrainians. Mykolaiv Is a Test Case.
2022-10-27.abstract: In a battered city on Ukraine’s southern coast, salt water runs from the taps and electricity is sporadic. Residents curse Russia, but also express frustration with their own leaders.

2022-10-27.title: Putin begins a high-profile address amid fears that he is escalating the war.
2022-10-27.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin began to deliver a wide-ranging speech on Thursday that was expected to offer an opportunity to gauge the Russian’s leader’s appetite for escalating the war in Ukraine to avoid defeat.

2022-10-27.title: Mexico’s Senate Votes to Eliminate Daylight Saving Time
2022-10-27.abstract: The measure, if signed by the president, would not affect the northern region of the country along the U.S. border.

2022-10-27.title: A Godfather of Chinese Nationalism Has Second Thoughts
2022-10-27.abstract: Wang Xiaodong, once called the standard-bearer of Chinese nationalism, now fends off criticisms of being too moderate, even a traitor. “They’ve forgotten,” he said, “I created them.”

2022-10-28.title: What Happened to Hu Jintao?
2022-10-28.abstract: We deconstruct video footage of the surprising moment when the former Chinese leader was escorted out of the highly choreographed Communist Party Congress.

2022-10-28.title: For Rishi Sunak, Family Wealth From Outsourcing Adds to a Secretive Fortune
2022-10-28.abstract: Britain’s new prime minister married into an $800 million stake in Infosys, a company that does not fit neatly into his party’s views on immigration.

2022-10-28.title: A government-connected Kremlin critic’s flight from Russia raises questions about who is still safe.
2022-10-28.abstract: Ksenia Sobchak entered Lithuania this week.

2022-10-28.title: A Celebrated Japanese Artist Died Trying to Save Others From Drowning
2022-10-28.abstract: Kazuki Takahashi, creator of the internationally famous Yu-Gi-Oh! manga, was found dead in July. At the time, officials did not disclose the circumstances.

2022-10-28.title: Win or Lose, Bolsonaro Has Destroyed Trust in Brazil’s Elections
2022-10-28.abstract: President Jair Bolsonaro has attacked Brazil’s electronic voting system. Now, ahead of Sunday’s elections, many of his supporters believe there will be fraud.

2022-10-29.title: After Years of ‘Hell’ in ISIS Detention Camp, 17 Australians Return Home
2022-10-29.abstract: Dozens more Australians, many of them children, are still being held in Syria, but there is hope that the government will soon sponsor more releases.

2022-10-29.title: At Least 45 Dead as Flooding and Landslides Hit the Philippines
2022-10-29.abstract: Severe Tropical Storm Nalgae made landfall early Saturday and was expected to continue bringing strong winds and torrential rain to large areas of the country through the weekend.

2022-10-29.title: Bolsonaro vs. Lula: Brazil Faces a Stark Choice With Huge Stakes
2022-10-29.abstract: Brazilians head to the polls on Sunday in an election between two political heavyweights that could have global repercussions.

2022-10-29.title: One in Five Canadians Is Now an Immigrant, and the Nation Approves
2022-10-29.abstract: Even as immigration has become increasingly divisive in many Western countries, particularly the United States, polls indicate that most Canadians support it.

2022-10-29.title: Here’s What to Know About Brazil’s Runoff Election
2022-10-29.abstract: The vote comes after a long and ugly campaign that pits two major political figures against each other in race that has tested the country’s democracy.

2022-10-30.title: At Least 151 Killed in Halloween Crowd Surge in Seoul
2022-10-30.abstract: As many as 100,000 people were celebrating in a popular nightlife district in the center of the South Korean capital.

2022-10-30.title: What to do in a crowd crush.
2022-10-30.abstract: Experts say that when a group is packed so close together that no one can move, their airways can become constricted. Victims can suffocate even while standing.

2022-10-30.title: Did Germany Learn From Its Russia Trouble? The Test May Come in China.
2022-10-30.abstract: On the eve of Chancellor Olaf Scholz’s first trip to Beijing, concerns are growing that economic dependence on China is leaving his country vulnerable — again.

2022-10-30.title: Witnesses describe the chaos in Itaewon.
2022-10-30.abstract: With officials unsure of what caused the disaster, recollections from people who were there are helping to paint a fuller picture.

2022-10-30.title: The crowd crush was like a ‘tsunami wave.’
2022-10-30.abstract: A witness described the force of the crowd pushing him back and forth near the scene of the disaster.

2022-10-31.title: Russia Is Urged to Rejoin Grain Deal as Hunger Warnings Mount
2022-10-31.abstract: Moscow dismissed accusations that it was using food as a weapon and said Ukraine was to blame for its decision to suspend an agreement that allowed for grain shipments.

2022-10-31.title: Bolsonaro lost. Will he accept the results?
Brazil Ejects Bolsonaro and Brings Back Leftist Former Leader Lula
2022-10-31.abstract: Brazilians voted out their far-right leader, Jair Bolsonaro, after a single term and replaced him with former President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva.

2022-10-31.title: Young Dreams Extinguished in the Crush of a Halloween Crowd in Seoul
2022-10-31.abstract: More than 100 of those killed in the South Korean capital were in their 20s, planning for the next chapter of their lives.
